extra.message: When running containers in Kubernetes, it's important to ensure that they  are properly secured to prevent privilege escalation attacks.  One potential vulnerability is when a container is allowed to run  applications as the root user, which could allow an attacker to gain  access to sensitive resources. To mitigate this risk, it's recommended to  add a `securityContext` to the container, with the parameter `runAsNonRoot`  set to `true`. This will ensure that the container runs as a non-root user,  limiting the damage that could be caused by any potential attacks. By  adding a `securityContext` to the container in your Kubernetes pod, you can  help to ensure that your containerized applications are more secure and  less vulnerable to privilege escalation attacks.

Internal matching error when running yaml.github-actions.security.curl-eval.curl-eval on .github\workflows\cd.yml:
 An error occurred while invoking the Semgrep engine. Please help us fix this by creating an issue at https://github.com/semgrep/semgrep

rule yaml.github-actions.security.curl-eval.curl-eval: metavariable-pattern failed when parsing $SHELL's content as Bash: set -euo pipefail
REGION="${{ vars.CLOUD_RUN_REGION }}"
mkdir -p artifacts/release
: > artifacts/release/production-rollout.tsv

while IFS='|' read -r component service image; do
  before_json="artifacts/release/${component}-before.json"
  after_json="artifacts/release/${component}-after.json"

  if ! gcloud run services describe "$service" --region "$REGION" --format=json > "$before_json" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo '{}' > "$before_json"
  fi

  previous_revision=$(node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const data=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(process.argv[1],'utf8')); const traffic=(data.status&&data.status.traffic)||[]; const active=traffic.find((item)=>Number(item.percent||0)>0); process.stdout.write(active?.revisionName||'');" "$before_json")

  gcloud run deploy "$service" --image "$image" --region "$REGION" --platform managed --tag candidate --no-traffic --quiet
  gcloud run services describe "$service" --region "$REGION" --format=json > "$after_json"

  candidate_revision=$(node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const data=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(process.argv[1],'utf8')); process.stdout.write(data.status?.latestCreatedRevisionName||'');" "$after_json")
  candidate_url=$(node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const data=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(process.argv[1],'utf8')); const traffic=(data.status&&data.status.traffic)||[]; const entry=traffic.find((item)=>item.tag==='candidate'); process.stdout.write(entry?.url||'');" "$after_json")

  echo "${component}_previous_revision=${previous_revision}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
  echo "${component}_candidate_revision=${candidate_revision}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
  echo "${component}_candidate_url=${candidate_url}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
  printf '%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\n' "$component" "$service" "$previous_revision" "$candidate_revision" "$can... (truncated 1167 more characters)

Internal matching error when running yaml.github-actions.security.curl-eval.curl-eval on .github\workflows\cd.yml:
 An error occurred while invoking the Semgrep engine. Please help us fix this by creating an issue at https://github.com/semgrep/semgrep

rule yaml.github-actions.security.curl-eval.curl-eval: metavariable-pattern failed when parsing $SHELL's content as Bash: set -euo pipefail
REGION="${{ vars.CLOUD_RUN_REGION }}"
mkdir -p artifacts/release
: > artifacts/release/staging-rollout.tsv

while IFS='|' read -r component service image; do
  before_json="artifacts/release/${component}-before.json"
  after_json="artifacts/release/${component}-after.json"

  if ! gcloud run services describe "$service" --region "$REGION" --format=json > "$before_json" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo '{}' > "$before_json"
  fi

  previous_revision=$(node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const data=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(process.argv[1],'utf8')); const traffic=(data.status&&data.status.traffic)||[]; const active=traffic.find((item)=>Number(item.percent||0)>0); process.stdout.write(active?.revisionName||'');" "$before_json")

  gcloud run deploy "$service" --image "$image" --region "$REGION" --platform managed --tag candidate --no-traffic --quiet
  gcloud run services describe "$service" --region "$REGION" --format=json > "$after_json"

  candidate_revision=$(node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const data=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(process.argv[1],'utf8')); process.stdout.write(data.status?.latestCreatedRevisionName||'');" "$after_json")
  candidate_url=$(node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const data=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(process.argv[1],'utf8')); const traffic=(data.status&&data.status.traffic)||[]; const entry=traffic.find((item)=>item.tag==='candidate'); process.stdout.write(entry?.url||'');" "$after_json")

  gcloud run services update-traffic "$service" --region "$REGION" --to-revisions "${candidate_revision}=100" --quiet
  printf '%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\n' "$component" "$service" "$previous_revision" "$candidate_revision" "$candidate_url" >> artifacts/release/staging-rollout.tsv
done <<EOF
